VIX 18.65 (low but firm; a fresh read that firmed back up from the pre-close 18.02, ~flat vs Friday's 18.77, still above ~16.4-16.7 on 7/16), SPY bull-stacked but back on its 50-day and -2.05% off the ATH, and QQQ 696.06 TANGLED and -3.13% below its 50-day. The subtle danger is COMPLACENCY dressed as rotation: the crowd treats cool CPI as an all-clear on the Fed, but the 7/29 hike tail (carried ~46.5%, STALE/UNVERIFIED) is a live two-way risk into still-unverified demand data. That is the underpriced near-term asymmetry. What would FALSIFY the read: a VIX push >19-20 or term-structure inversion (regime flip), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~743.4) that fails to reclaim (bull failure), a hot retail-sales/claims print, the market pricing/delivering the ~46.5% 7/29 hike (the biggest single falsifier), or — bullishly — QQQ reclaiming its 50-day (~718.6) and restoring its bull-stack with positive 5-day and MSFT confirming a close holding its 50-day (a broadening that ends the lone crack).
